UC-001.Action: Exports Telegram group chat via Telegram Desktop and provides the JSON export path
UC-001.Goal: To supply raw chat data for extraction
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: Application accepts a folder path or result.json file and validates its format

UC-002.Action: Enters OpenRouter API key (or provides it via .env) and selects a free LLM model
UC-002.Goal: To authenticate with the LLM provider and choose the best available free model
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: API key loaded from .env > env var > config.json; model selector shows benchmarked free models; choice persisted

UC-003.Action: Parses result.json into a list of Message objects
UC-003.Goal: To normalize Telegram export data into a uniform internal format
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: All text messages extracted; rich text flattened; service/empty messages skipped

UC-004.Action: Groups messages into batches by count and token limit
UC-004.Goal: To prepare optimal payloads for LLM API calls
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Batches respect batch_size and max_tokens limits

UC-005.Action: Sends message batches to OpenRouter API using a user-selected system prompt and parses structured JSON responses
UC-005.Goal: To extract data from chat messages using LLM with configurable extraction logic
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: Extraction driven by system prompt from prompts/ folder; {type, value} pairs; retry on 429/timeout/invalid JSON

UC-006.Action: Writes extracted data to JSON file with metadata including the prompt used
UC-006.Goal: To produce structured JSON output with full traceability
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: JSON contains extraction_meta (source, counts, date, prompt text) and contacts array

UC-007.Action: Converts JSON output to formatted Excel with type normalization, auto-columns, and summary sheet
UC-007.Goal: To produce a human-readable table with all extracted data
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Excel has dynamic columns from discovered types; zebra striping; auto-filter; summary sheet with stats and prompt

UC-008.Action: Selects, edits, and saves system prompts via GUI tab or CLI flag
UC-008.Goal: To customize what data is extracted without changing code
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: Prompts stored as .txt in prompts/; GUI has editor tab with load/save/select; CLI accepts --prompt flag

UC-009.Action: Launches tabbed GUI (CustomTkinter) or CLI, monitors progress, opens JSON/Excel results
UC-009.Goal: To process exports with visual feedback and dual output
UC-009.AcceptanceCriteria: GUI tabs: Extraction + Prompt; dual output buttons (JSON, Excel); CLI with progress bar; .exe packaging

UC-010.Action: Runs benchmark to compare speed and quality of free LLM models
UC-010.Goal: To choose the optimal model for extraction
UC-010.AcceptanceCriteria: Benchmark tests all free models on sample data; reports time, contact count, types; recommends best model
